description: Sequentially checks each element in a list until the target is found or the list ends.
Efficiently finds an element in a sorted array by repeatedly dividing the search interval in half.
Repeatedly swaps adjacent elements if they are in the wrong order until the array is sorted.
Finds the smallest element and places it at the beginning, repeating for remaining elements.
Builds a sorted array one element at a time by inserting elements into correct position.
Divides the array into halves, sorts them recursively, then merges sorted halves.
Uses a pivot element to partition the array and recursively sorts partitions.
Finds two numbers in an array that add up to a target value using hashing or brute force.
Generates Fibonacci numbers using recursive relation: F(n)=F(n-1)+F(n-2).
Computes n! by multiplying all integers from 1 to n recursively or iteratively.
